name: exec
label: exec
description: Run TypeScript ESM code using Node.js from current directory. Returns stdout and stderr. If truncated, full output is saved to a temporary file.
Optionally provide npm packages to make available for import in the code and/or a timeout in seconds.
This tool follows CodeAct principles, it let the agent (you) write and run code that uses tools.
Write code that calls `call_tool("name", ...)` to call a tool.
promptSnippet: Run TypeScript code with programmatic tools and optional npm packages
promptGuidelines: Use `exec` to execute code with programmatic tools.
Use the `packages` argument to specify the required npm packages you will need in your code.
Constrain stdout/stderr to contain only the necessary data you need to know for the current task.

packages.description: List of npm packages to make available for import in the code, for example ["yaml@2.8.3", "zod@4.3.6", "lodash"]. If no version specifier is provided, defaults to the latest version.

code.description: TypeScript code to run. Top-level await and imports from requested packages are supported.

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds. If omitted, defaults to {{defaultTimeout}}.
